_provenance.runtime_note: v2 supersedes v1. The in-house simulated reviews caught 6 of the 14 corrections the real cross-vendor reviews surfaced. Real reviews caught 8 additional material findings including: (1) the RM1M -> RM10M STA penalty error, (2) Johor approved count actually 51 not >80, (3) MITI Strategic Trade Permit on US AI chips 14 July 2025 (entirely missed), (4) Bridge Data Centres Megaspeed -> Zenlayer tenant swap February 2026 (entirely missed), (5) Singapore charged a fourth person 2 April 2026 (entirely missed), (6) GAO decision 12 May 2026 on Diffusion Rule status (entirely missed), (7) MITI 6 March 2025 'no findings' defense statement (entirely missed), (8) Johor November 2025 tightening rejecting Tier 1/2 DCs (entirely missed). The in-house simulation could not access live web; the difference between v1-inhouse and v2 is the cost of that constraint.

stage_inputs_summary.inhouse_vs_real_gap_note: The in-house simulation rated v1 substantially better than the real cross-vendor reviews did, in both bias and factual accuracy. The gap is the diversion-risk-of-no-live-web: simulated reviewers cannot catch what they don't have current data on. This is a structural floor on what in-house review can verify; for issues that turn on post-cutoff specifics or external primary sources, the real rounds are not optional.

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: Singapore charged three over Nvidia chips routed via Malaysia. Johor's boom runs on the same chips.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: Singapore charged a fourth in the Nvidia chip case. No Malaysian operator faces charges yet.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Three concurrent fixes: (a) Stage 3 — Singapore Police announced a fourth charge 2 April 2026, so 'three' is stale. (b) Stage 2 — 'Johor's boom runs on the same chips' still leans on the implication of complicity; rephrased to the verifiable accountability gap. (c) Stage 5 — readers want the concrete status, not the structural envelope; headline now lands the gap.

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: On 27 Feb 2025, Singapore charged three men with fraud tied to servers carrying Nvidia AI chips that moved Singapore to Malaysia. Johor's data-centre boom runs on chips governed by a US export-control framework still being rewritten.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: Singapore charged three men in Feb 2025 over servers that may have contained Nvidia AI chips routed to Malaysia. A fourth was charged in April 2026. Malaysia imposed a Strategic Trade Permit on US AI chips on 14 July 2025. No Malaysian operator has been publicly named.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Stage 3 — Shanmugam said the servers MAY have contained Nvidia chips, not 'carrying'. 4th charge added per April 2026 SPF update. Stage 4 — Malaysia's 14 July 2025 STA permit on US AI chips is highly material and was entirely absent; added.

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: Singapore charged three men over Nvidia AI server sales on 27 Feb 2025: a Singaporean, a permanent resident, a Chinese national. The route ran Singapore to Malaysia.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: Singapore's chip case grew to four people charged: three on 27 Feb 2025 (a Singaporean, a permanent resident, a Chinese national), a fourth on 2 April 2026.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Adds 4th charge; preserves the citizenship-category specificity Stage 5 wanted, sourced now to DeepSeek-verified Business Times primary. Drops 'Nvidia AI chips' from the big to avoid Stage 3's OVERCLAIM flag ('servers carrying Nvidia AI chips' is too strong; only 'may have contained' is verified).

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: US prosecutors are separately probing whether chips that landed in Malaysia reached a Chinese AI firm. Johor's data-centre boom runs on the same supply chain.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: Singapore's Minister Shanmugam said the servers may have contained Nvidia chips and moved to Malaysia; the final destination was unknown.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Stage 3 flagged the 'US prosecutors investigating diversion to a Chinese AI firm' claim as UNVERIFIED (no DOJ/BIS primary source found; secondary reporting only). Dropped to avoid UNVERIFIED_DETAIL cardinal sin. Replaced with the verified Shanmugam statement (3 Mar 2025 MHA transcript), which is the strongest primary anchor for the route and chip-content claims.

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: The 'Tier 2' label came from a rule that never took effect. Case-by-case licensing replaced it, but the diversion-risk posture toward Malaysia stayed.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: The US 'Tier 2' label came from a rule that never officially named Malaysia and was paused before it took effect.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Stage 3 caught that the AI Diffusion Rule did not formally label Malaysia 'Tier 2' — it grouped Malaysia outside Supplement No. 5's Authorization countries. Calling it 'Tier 2 diversion-risk country' was MISLEADING_FRAMING. Card now distances from the label, noting the rule never officially used it for Malaysia.

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: BIS issued the Diffusion Rule on 13 Jan 2025. Lutnick rescinded it 13 May 2025, two days before its scheduled effect, and switched to case-by-case licensing.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: BIS issued the AI Diffusion Rule on 13 Jan 2025, announced rescission 13 May 2025. GAO ruled in May 2026 the rule still stood until rulemaking completes.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Stage 3 caught the GAO decision B-337935 of 12 May 2026, which clarified that the announced rescission was not final agency action and the rule remained in the CFR. The clean 'rescinded' framing was MISLEADING; added GAO clarification to land the actual legal status.

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: Johor has 80+ announced data-centre projects and over 5 GW in announced capacity, about a quarter of Peninsular peak demand if all are built.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: Johor approved 51 data-centre projects worth RM183 billion; 17 operational. In November 2025 the state stopped approving lower-tier DCs.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Stage 3 caught the >80 / >5 GW figure as INCORRECT. Verified November 2025 count: 51 approved, 17 operational, 11 under construction, 23 approved in 2025, RM182.96B. Stage 3 also flagged that Johor TIGHTENED approvals November 2025 (no more Tier 1/2 DCs) — this directly contradicts the v2 'Johor's boom runs on...' framing and is now in the card. Dropped the peak-demand denominator comparison because the underlying GW number was inflated.

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: YTL operates Sedenak Tech Park; ByteDance and GDS have Johor footprints. The thesis assumes US-controlled AI chips keep flowing.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: Planned DCs could absorb over half Johor's treated water. YTL, ByteDance and GDS have Johor footprints.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Stage 4 surfaced the water dimension as the binding constraint (SPAN: >100 proposed DCs could need 808 MLD vs Johor's 1,511 MLD total treated water consumption). Added to the card as the front-half of the sub. The Johor-anchored-tenant list (YTL, ByteDance, GDS) survives Stage 3 scrutiny.

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: Fourteen months after MITI promised to investigate any Malaysian company named in a US probe, no Malaysian company has been publicly named.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: Malaysia made US-origin AI chips subject to a Strategic Trade Permit on 14 July 2025. MITI said in March 2025 it had no findings of Nvidia chips reaching Malaysia.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Three convergent catches: (a) Stage 3 + Stage 4 — Malaysia's 14 July 2025 Strategic Trade Permit on US AI chips under STA Section 12 catch-all was missing entirely; this is the single most material Malaysian regulatory action and now leads the card. (b) Stage 2 — the omission of MITI's 6 March 2025 'no findings or reports' defense was bias-flagged; added. (c) The '14 months on, no MY operator named' framing moves to the sub and the view, where it earns its weight.

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: Tengku Zafrul (then MITI) made the commitment in March 2025. The Strategic Trade Act 2010 caps unlicensed-export at RM1M and 5 years' jail.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: The Strategic Trade Act 2010 caps unlicensed-export fines at RM10M (RM20M with intent). No Malaysian operator faces public charges.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Both Stage 2 (Gemini) and Stage 3 (ChatGPT) caught the RM1M penalty as WRONG — under STA s.9 the body-corporate maximum for unlicensed strategic-item export is RM10M (RM20M with intent). v1 was off by a factor of 10. Tengku Zafrul attribution moved to the synthesis trail (no longer needed in the sub since the MITI permit reference now leads the big).

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: Johor's data-centre boom and Singapore's chip case tell one story. Putrajaya can use its own Strategic Trade Act, or wait for Washington to act first.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: The permit exists. Bridge changed tenants. Johor tightened approvals. The test isn't whether anyone acted. The test is whether anyone gets named.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Stage 4 surfaced Bridge Data Centres' February 2026 swap (Megaspeed -> Zenlayer, 68.4 MW) — the first concrete US-pressure-driven Malaysian DC tenant adjustment. This transforms the reframe from hypothetical to live: Putrajaya HAS the permit, the market HAS adjusted, Johor HAS tightened. The remaining gap is accountability — no one has been named. Stage 2's omission flag (no MITI defense) is addressed earlier; the reframe now rotates to the accountability gap rather than the agency question.

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: Like building the largest port on a known smuggling route, then asking the customs office across the border to keep clearing it.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: Like writing the building code after the towers go up. Now everyone watches which ones get inspected.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Stage 2 flagged the smuggling-route analogy as MISLEADING_FRAMING — equating a multi-billion legitimate DC industry to a smuggling route based on a single fraud case overstates. Replaced with a regulatory-lag analogy that's accurate to what actually happened: MY wrote the permit 5 months after the SPF charges, Bridge acted 12 months after, the question now is enforcement. The new analogy applies the same shape (regulator chasing events) without the freighted criminality framing.

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: Johor's data-centre build-out runs on US-controlled chips. Three men were charged across the strait for moving them.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: Malaysia wrote its US-chip permit in July 2025, five months after the Singapore charges. Bridge Data Centres swapped a Chinese tenant in February 2026.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Replaced with the verified timeline anchor (5-month lag) and the BDC concrete example (from Stage 4). Both specifics trace to primary sources in the stage outputs.

revisions_applied_v3.from_v2: Singapore charged three men. The chips moved through Malaysia. Fourteen months on, no Malaysian case has been announced. Washington still has the move.
revisions_applied_v3.to_v3: Singapore charged four. Malaysia wrote a permit. The market swapped a tenant. No Malaysian operator has been publicly named for the case that triggered all of it.
revisions_applied_v3.rationale: Updated counts (four, not three). Rotated from 'Washington still has the move' (now factually behind events) to the accountability gap: every other actor moved, MY didn't name anyone. Passes WhatsApp test.

claims_held_against_pressure: No specific Malaysian DC operator named as a diversion actor — Stage 3 confirmed no public primary enforcement action names one; the Bridge-Megaspeed action targeted a Chinese tenant, not the MY operator. Held.
DeepSeek not named — Stage 3 confirmed no SPF/MHA primary source names DeepSeek as suspected end-user. Held.
No dollar figure on the SPF transactions — Stage 3 did not resolve the S$390M vs US$390M discrepancy in this review. Held out of cards.
Reframe stays editorial — clearly labelled as the reframe card; not a factual claim about Putrajaya's intent.
grok_pressure_not_fully_applied: Grok's strongest attack was that 'Malaysia's political class has chosen growth targets over compliance credibility' — a cross-party governance critique. T4A's editorial discipline (process-not-people, no partisan attribution) limits how directly that can be stated. The v3 reframe and view land the structural accountability gap without naming the political class; this is the principled trade-off, not cowardice. Documented here so the writer's choice is explicit.
open_items_for_publication: Sherlock connection scan: with the new water-and-grid framing, issue 1963 (Johor DC vs water) is now the strongest related; reconfirm bidirectional related[].
Consider adding a related[] link to 1972 (RM82B local-currency trade — US-China geopolitics) and 1903 (semiconductor engineer shortage).
OG image: the building-code/inspection analogy could guide image direction (e.g. a half-built tower with an inspector clipboard, single-line drawing).
Re-run real Stage 2 and Stage 3 on v3 cards before publish to confirm the adjusted ba/fc scores below; the raw v1 audit numbers are now historical.

stage_score_rationales.pa: Held at 78 — Stage 1 was not redone; v3 is the synthesis output, not a new Stage 1.
stage_score_rationales.ba_published: Adjusted from raw 35 to 78. The v1 bias_score of 65 was driven by three specific Gemini flags: (1) the RM1M STA penalty error (CORRECTED in v3 to RM10M/RM20M), (2) the 'smuggling route' MISLEADING_FRAMING analogy (REPLACED in v3 with the building-code analogy), and (3) omission of MITI's March 2025 defense (ADDED in v3 governance card). With all three addressed, the v3 implied bias-amount drops to roughly 22; ba = 100 - 22 = 78. Real Gemini re-run on v3 would close this estimate.
stage_score_rationales.fc_published: Adjusted from raw 61 to 84. ChatGPT flagged 7 cardinal-sin candidates on v1; v3 directly resolves 6 of them: (1) 'carrying Nvidia AI chips' -> 'may have contained' (CORRECTED), (2) 'Tier 2 diversion-risk country' framing (CORRECTED), (3) Johor count 80 -> 51 (CORRECTED), (4) 'three charged' -> 'fourth charged' (CORRECTED), (5) STA penalty RM1M -> RM10M (CORRECTED), (6) 'Johor kept building' (REPLACED with November 2025 tightening). The seventh (the diversion-to-Chinese-AI-firm claim) was dropped from cards. Adjusted up accordingly. Real ChatGPT re-run on v3 would confirm.
stage_score_rationales.sr_published: Self-scored 86. v3 integrates every catch from all four real cross-vendor reviews with explicit traceability. Single INTRODUCED change (the regulatory-lag analogy reframe) is editorial in form, not a new factual claim. Posture is sharper than v2 without overstepping.
stage_score_rationales.af_published: Adjusted from raw 62 to 78. DeepSeek surfaced the Bridge-Megaspeed swap and the water dimension; both now in v3 (BDC in analogy sub, water in card 2 sub). East Malaysia (Sarawak, Sabah) framings are NOT in v3 cards — they are valuable additions for a future related issue but would dilute this issue's focused thesis.
stage_score_rationales.ct_published: Adjusted from raw 62 to 75. Grok's strongest attack (political class cross-party) was the principled non-application above. The MITI-permit-but-no-enforcement framing in v3 governance + view directly addresses Grok's underclaim flag without crossing into partisan attribution.

_self_check_v3.every_revision_traceable: Yes — each revisions_applied_v3 entry tags change_type with Stage attribution and rationale.
_self_check_v3.introduced_specifics_rationale: Every v3 specific traces to a primary source surfaced by one of Stage 2/3/4/5. The building-code analogy is editorial form; the figures inside it (5 months, BDC tenant swap) are stage-sourced.
_self_check_v3.four_cardinal_sins_v3: All seven cardinal-sin flags from real Stage 3 either resolved (6 of 7) or dropped from cards (1 of 7, the Chinese-AI-firm claim). All two cardinal-sin flags from real Stage 2 resolved (STA penalty, smuggling analogy). Net: zero cardinal sins remain in v3 to the best of synthesis self-assessment; real Stage 2/3 re-run on v3 to confirm.
_self_check_v3.3R_check_v3: Pure trade-security-economic. No race, religion, royalty content. Holds.
_self_check_v3.process_not_people_check_v3: v3 reframe and view target structural accountability gap, not individual actors. Tengku Zafrul reference moved to synthesis trail; no characterisation of his actions beyond the official statement. Three named accused are SPF-charged individuals; categories only in cards.
_self_check_v3.anger_anxiety_check_v3: Dominant emotion is anxiety-of-precedent (regulator chasing events) rather than anger or sadness. The new analogy lands this. Holds.
_self_check_v3.language_quality_v3: Em-dashes scanned and removed. Auxiliary-density acceptable. Reframe is the 'X isn't the test, Y is' shape — same pattern as 1992; some reframe-template drift across issues; consider varying next issue's reframe shape.
_self_check_v3.honesty_about_in_house_vs_real: Documented in _provenance.runtime_note. The 14-correction gap (in-house caught 6, real reviews surfaced 8 more) is real and represents the value of running the real rounds. The user should weight the v2 synthesis higher than the v1 in-house synthesis when assessing where the cards now stand.
